summary: Tested all 5 new UI features + backend regression after server.py git restore. Backend: 20/20 pytest passed (100%) — auth, /auth/me exposes bannerUrl, GET /rooms returns roomBanner Unsplash URLs (all 4 default rooms), PUT /users/profile accepts+persists bannerUrl, friends/notifications/games/leaderboard/search all green (incl. /leaderboard/active 200 — no regression of prior fix). Frontend: All 5 features verified — (1) Rooms screen renders 3-column grid with banner images per room (Unsplash images load, occupancy chip X/36 visible, progress bar) — confirmed via card x-positions [16,138,260] proving 3 cols. (2) Room screen layout = header → Host-a-Game → messages list → 'IN THE ROOM (6/36)' section with 'Hold & drag your avatar' hint → 6 draggable members → input bar (correct top→bottom ordering, members at y=658 above input y=778). (3) Profile screen has banner area, 110px avatar with camera-icon overlay, Edit Banner button, display name, @username, Coins/XP/Level stats, Bio, Email, Logout, edit pencil. (4) Bottom-nav Profile tab shows person icon for Alice (no photoUrl) — correct fallback. (5) Member testIDs (member-{userId}) and room-card testIDs (room-card-{id}) all wired. No red-screen errors. Minor: 'shadow*' and 'props.pointerEvents' deprecation warnings in console; DraggableMember has a style conflict — when dragging, the `dragging` StyleSheet applies `transform:[{scale:1.1}]` which collides with the inline `transform: pan.getTranslateTransform()`, so the scale-up visual on drag will be lost (function visually subtle, drag itself still works). No deprecated expo-av/etc packages found in this iteration's changes.

ui_bugs.issue: `styles.dragging` sets `transform: [{ scale: 1.1 }]`, but the Animated.View already receives an inline `transform: pan.getTranslateTransform()`. Inline style transform overrides the StyleSheet transform, so the scale-up visual when picking up the avatar never actually applies. Use a separate Animated value for scale and compose with pan transform array. Ref: /app/frontend/src/components/DraggableMember.tsx:117

issues: Console warns: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow (multiple components use shadowColor/shadowOffset/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius — e.g., DraggableMember.tsx). Replace with boxShadow for web compatibility.

react_native_issues.issue: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated on RN Web — use boxShadow. Reference: /app/frontend/src/components/DraggableMember.tsx:118-122 (likely others in profile/room screens).

'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — appears in console after navigation; somewhere a component is passing pointerEvents as a prop instead of via style. Reference: not file-pinpointed, search frontend codebase for `pointerEvents=` props.

action_items: (LOW) DraggableMember: split the `dragging` scale into an Animated.Value composed with the pan transform array so the visual cue appears on touch. Current code path: styles.dragging.transform is silently overridden.
(LOW/cosmetic) Migrate all shadow* style props to boxShadow on web — see DraggableMember.tsx and any other components for RN Web compatibility.
(LOW/cosmetic) Find the component passing `pointerEvents` as a prop and move it to `style.pointerEvents` to remove the deprecation warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1410 lines — should be split into routers (auth, rooms, messages, friends, notifications, games, leaderboard, profile). Carrying this comment forward from iteration_3.
Update /init/rooms could be made idempotent on banner-only updates — currently it skips entirely if any room exists, so changing default banner URLs in code requires a manual DB update.

RoomCard styles `maxWidth: '33%'` works but combined with FlatList numColumns=3 + columnWrapperStyle.gap=8, the third card may very occasionally overflow on narrow widths. Consider computing card width = (screenWidth - paddings - gaps)/3 explicitly.

DraggableMember bounds (boundsWidth/boundsHeight) are passed as props but no parent file is seen here — verify the parent computes them after layout and not as a fixed value, otherwise dragging will clamp incorrectly.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 5 features visually verified, 1 LOW style bug)

seed_data_creation: No new seed users. test_update_profile_with_bannerUrl set alice's bannerUrl to a fake Unsplash URL — cleared via direct Mongo $unset after the run (alice.bannerUrl is now null again).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (https://vip-styling-suite.preview.emergentagent.com). Tests fallback to http://localhost:8001. The regression test file /app/backend/tests/test_ui_features_regression.py is idempotent and asserts bannerUrl/roomBanner presence — run it first on any future server.py change. Frontend: testIDs in place: room-card-{id}, member-{userId}, notifications-bell. Bottom nav Profile tab is at approx (341, 819) on 390x844 viewport — `.click()` on the text 'Profile' may need force=True or coordinate click due to overflow:hidden text wrappers. Drag testing on web preview is NOT meaningful — PanResponder works but mouse-based drag-and-drop in Playwright fires pan callbacks unreliably; verify on a real device or Expo Go.
rca_of_issue: No P1/P2 bugs to RCA this iteration. The DraggableMember scale-on-drag style override is a known RN gotcha: when an Animated.View receives both an inline `transform` and a stylesheet `transform`, the inline one wins — RN does not merge transform arrays. Fix: keep pan-driven translate inline, drive scale via a second Animated.Value, and compose: `transform: [...pan.getTranslateTransform(), { scale }]`.
